title: Attention over set members
learningObjectives: Explain attention over set members in plain language
Write down the objective and identify its assumptions
Design one test that could falsify an over-strong interpretation

whyThisLesson.text: Attention lets a set encoder weigh relationships between members instead of treating every member as equally informative. This lesson gives you a concrete test for that claim before the course moves to the next abstraction.

intuition.analogy: A bowl of fruit has members but no first apple. A good set representation should not change when you stir the bowl. Attention lets a set encoder weigh relationships between members instead of treating every member as equally informative.
intuition.analogyLimit: Permutation invariance is necessary for sets but it does not guarantee robustness to bad members or preserve all relations.
intuition.text: Attention lets a set encoder weigh relationships between members instead of treating every member as equally informative.

toyExample.description: Make sets from the same latent class, permute them, then inject unrelated members. Compare mean pooling with a learned aggregation rule.

steps.result: Specify exactly what attention over set members should recover, preserve, or ignore.
steps.insight: The intended factor must be known before a toy result can be judged.

steps.result: Use the same data, capacity, training budget, and evaluation examples for both methods.
steps.insight: Otherwise a visible gain may be a resource difference in disguise.

steps.result: Vary the source correlation, noise, view quality, or sparsity and record the first failure.
steps.insight: The boundary of success is usually more useful than the headline score.

visual.question: Which assumption behind attention over set members changes the result most?
visual.description: Move one control at a time. Treat the display as a toy model, then write down the real experiment it suggests.

technical.text: Attention lets a set encoder weigh relationships between members instead of treating every member as equally informative. In practice, write down the input unit, the operation applied to it, and the exact statistic used to score the result. Those are different things. A learned representation can be useful even when the interpretation attached to it turns out to be wrong.

mathematics.explanation: For this lesson, the expression makes the central trade-off explicit. Attention lets a set encoder weigh relationships between members instead of treating every member as equally informative.
mathematics.whyNeeded: It says precisely what the method is being rewarded for.
mathematics.ifRemoved: You could not tell whether two implementations were solving the same problem.
mathematics.encourages: A testable prediction about the chosen objective.
mathematics.doesNotGuarantee: Permutation invariance is necessary for sets but it does not guarantee robustness to bad members or preserve all relations.

methodComparison.question: How are members combined?
methodComparison.practicalImplication: Choose the simplest aggregator that passes the required robustness checks.

evidence.claim: Attention lets a set encoder weigh relationships between members instead of treating every member as equally informative.
evidence.note: Course explanation. Use the research library's primary sources for paper-specific empirical claims.

failureModes.name: A score that answers the wrong question
failureModes.mechanism: The objective rewards a proxy while the paper describes a stronger scientific property.
failureModes.symptom: The headline metric rises, but seed stability, controls, or interventions disagree.
failureModes.diagnosticTest: Add a matched negative control and one counterfactual intervention.
failureModes.mitigation: Either narrow the claim or add the missing measurement.
failureModes.setconcaRelevance: Report both the intended benefit and the most plausible shortcut for every SetConCA loss term.

setconcaConnection.relevance: Attention over set members determines how SetConCA should represent, group, or evaluate activation views.
setconcaConnection.whichPart: The choice of sparse code, view set, coordination loss, or evaluation.
setconcaConnection.whichDesignChoice: Match the pointwise SAE and SetConCA on model, activation site, width, sparsity budget, and compute.
setconcaConnection.whichFailure: Mistaking stronger agreement for recovery of the intended concept.
setconcaConnection.whichExperiment: Compare fidelity, feature stability, selectivity, and controlled interventions; report each result separately.
setconcaConnection.alternativeExplanation: An apparent gain may come from extra context, effective capacity, or easier positives rather than set coordination.

exercises.prompt: A paper claims that attention over set members improved interpretability. What one result would you ask for before accepting that claim?
exercises.reveal: Ask for the smallest missing control: a matched baseline, several seeds, a negative control, or an intervention that tests the claimed mechanism.

masteryQuestions.prompt: Explain attention over set members to a colleague, then name one thing the method cannot establish by itself.

nextLesson.bridge: Next: Set statistics and intruder robustness. The next idea reuses this lesson's representation and makes a new assumption explicit.

fields.label: Falsifiable claim
fields.prompt: Write the smallest claim this lesson supports.

fields.label: Control
fields.prompt: What control would rule out a shortcut?
